default_recommendation.if_stronger_qmsum_semantic_claim_is_need: Prefer Option B or Option D.

options.default_when: Phase 2 should continue with deterministic-only methodology.
options.description: Do not perform additional review. Preserve QMSum as deterministic proxy/risk evidence with explicit residual caveats.
options.name: No semantic judge; freeze caveat
options.risk: Does not strengthen QMSum semantic-quality language.

options.benefit: Strongest non-LLM review path for a small bounded sample.
options.default_when: The user wants a stronger QMSum semantic claim without introducing an LLM judge.
options.description: Have a human apply the fixed rubric to all six residual rows using the review packet.
options.name: Human review on six target rows
options.risk: Adds human-review methodology that must be disclosed and may not scale.

options.benefit: Can provide consistent structured labels if judge setup is accepted.
options.default_when: Only after explicit approval for an LLM judge path.
options.description: Run a controlled LLM judge only after explicit approval, using the frozen rubric and packet.
options.name: LLM judge with fixed rubric and evidence packet
options.risk: Introduces judge-model bias and must not be treated as ground truth.

options.benefit: Minimizes human-review load while targeting the hardest residual rows.
options.default_when: The user wants stronger semantic evidence while keeping review small.
options.description: Review only rows whose deterministic labels remain unresolved or conflicting, while freezing clear failures as caveated.
options.name: Hybrid human review only unresolved/conflicting rows
options.risk: Requires a careful pre-declared unresolved/conflict selection rule.
